properties.name: The Shire
properties.description: Home of the Hobbits. A peaceful, rolling countryside with green hills and comfortable holes.

properties.name: Hobbiton
properties.description: Principal town of the Shire where Bilbo and Frodo lived.

properties.name: Bree
properties.description: A settlement of Big Folk and hobbits, home to the Prancing Pony inn.

properties.name: Weathertop (Amon Sûl)
properties.description: A prominent hilltop overlooking the landscape, site of an ancient watch-tower.

properties.name: Rivendell (Imladris)
properties.description: A hidden elven city founded by Elrond, a refuge of peace and wisdom.

properties.name: Moria (Khazad-dûm)
properties.description: Vast underground kingdom of dwarven mines, once great but now abandoned and dangerous.

properties.name: Lothlórien
properties.description: Golden woods of the Elves ruled by Galadriel, filled with mallorn trees.

properties.name: Amon Hen
properties.description: Hill of the Eye, location where the Fellowship broke apart.

properties.name: Isengard
properties.description: Stronghold of Saruman, surrounded by the tower Orthanc, now in ruins.

properties.name: Rohan
properties.description: Kingdom of the Rohirrim, a noble kingdom of horses and warriors.

properties.name: Edoras
properties.description: Capital city of Rohan, seat of the kings of the Rohirrim.

properties.name: Helm's Deep
properties.description: Great fortress of Rohan, a stronghold carved into the mountains.

properties.name: Gondor
properties.description: Great kingdom of Men, once mighty but fallen into shadow.

properties.name: Minas Tirith
properties.description: Capital of Gondor, the white city with seven levels built into a mountainside.

properties.name: Dol Amroth
properties.description: Coastal city of Gondor, ruled by Prince Imrahil, known for its white swans.

properties.name: Mordor
properties.description: Domain of Sauron, land of darkness and despair, guarded by mountains of fire.

properties.name: Mount Doom
properties.description: The fiery mountain where the One Ring was forged and must be destroyed.

properties.name: Barad-dûr
properties.description: The Dark Tower of Sauron, an awful structure that dominates Mordor.

properties.name: Mirkwood
properties.description: Dark and dangerous forest, home to giant spiders and other fell creatures.

properties.name: Erebor (Lonely Mountain)
properties.description: Great mountain kingdom of dwarves, home to vast treasures and Smaug's hoard.

properties.name: Lake-town (Esgaroth)
properties.description: Town of Men built on the waters of Long Lake, trade center between the Free Peoples.

properties.name: Tharbad
properties.description: Ancient ruined city on the Greyflood, once a great center of trade.

properties.name: Misty Mountains
properties.description: Great range of mountains, home to goblin strongholds and ancient evil.

properties.name: Grey Havens (Mithlond)
properties.description: Coastal port in the west where Elves depart Middle-earth to sail to Valinor.

properties.name: The Dead Marshes
properties.description: Desolate marshes filled with the restless dead and evil creatures.

properties.name: Anduin (Great River)
properties.description: Mightiest river of Middle-earth, flowing from north to south.

properties.name: Osgiliath
properties.description: Ancient capital of Gondor, now ruined and abandoned on the banks of Anduin.

properties.name: Minas Morgul
properties.description: Fortress of the Ringwraiths, pale and terrible, guarding the pass of Cirith Ungol.
